name: Wild Mushroom and Salmon Salad
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the canned salmon and set aside the juice.
2. Flake the fish into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
3. Divide the curly endive among 4 or 6 serving plates.
4. In a small pan, combine the reserved salmon juice with juniper berries, allspice berries, and white wine. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce to a simmer for 3 minutes. Allow it to cool.
5. Discard the berries and use the remaining stock from the pan.
6. Heat the walnut oil in a frying pan. Add the wild mushrooms, garlic, and onion. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are just tender; remove them from the pan and keep warm on a separate plate.
7. Pour the reserved stock into the pan with the oil. Cook rapidly over high heat for about 1 minute.
8. Return the cooked salmon flakes and vegetables to the pan. Gently combine everything, then pour over the endive in the serving plates.
9. Serve immediately.
Serves 4-6. Approximately 150 calories per serving
From: On the Wild Side - Alaska Canned Salmon Recipes
Reprinted with permission from Alaska Seafood Marketing Institute Meal-Master compatible recipe format courtesy of Karen Mintzias
